pi_items.source: Live test confirmed: 'mysql -u ga -ppassword123 sakila -N -e "SHOW TABLES LIKE 'payment'"' returns 'payment'. The Sakila database is the official MySQL sample database (https://dev.mysql.com/doc/sakila/en/). setup_task.sh queries sakila database directly via pymysql.

setup_task.sh drops 'processor_transactions' at line 33. export_result.sh checks information_schema.TABLES for TABLE_NAME='processor_transactions'. task.json description specifies 'sakila.processor_transactions'.

setup_task.sh drops 'v_payment_reconciliation' at line 36. export_result.sh checks information_schema.VIEWS for TABLE_NAME='v_payment_reconciliation' and queries it for per-category counts via GROUP BY match_status.
setup_task.sh drops 'sp_apply_corrections' at line 37. export_result.sh checks information_schema.ROUTINES for ROUTINE_NAME='sp_apply_corrections'.
setup_task.sh drops 'reconciliation_summary' at line 35. export_result.sh checks information_schema.TABLES for TABLE_NAME='reconciliation_summary' and queries its contents.
setup_task.sh drops 'audit_corrections' at line 34. export_result.sh checks information_schema.TABLES for TABLE_NAME='audit_corrections' and counts its rows.

Live test confirmed file exists: '-rw-r--r-- 1 ga ga 723974 Mar 22 02:22 /home/ga/Documents/imports/processor_transactions.csv'. File has 6719 data rows + 1 header = 6720 lines. Generated by setup_task.sh Python3 script from actual Sakila July 2005 payment data joined with customer emails.
export_result.sh checks this exact path at line 102: RECON_CSV='/home/ga/Documents/exports/reconciliation_report.csv'. Live test confirmed directory exists and is writable by ga user.
export_result.sh checks this exact path at line 103: SUMMARY_CSV='/home/ga/Documents/exports/reconciliation_summary.csv'. Live test confirmed directory exists and is writable by ga user.

setup_task.sh writes this file at line 227 via json.dump(). Live test confirmed contents: total_july_payments=6709, csv_data_rows=6719, internal_only_count=40, date_mismatch_count=50, amount_mismatch_count=35, duplicate_count=20, external_only_count=30, matched_count=6584.

setup_task.sh assigns these 6 categories deterministically: indices 0-39 -> INTERNAL_ONLY, 40-89 -> DATE_MISMATCH, 90-124 -> AMOUNT_MISMATCH, 125-144 -> DUPLICATE_EXTERNAL, rest -> MATCHED, plus 30 synthetic EXTERNAL_ONLY records. export_result.sh queries the view with GROUP BY match_status to count each category.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the standard MySQL Sakila sample database (https://dev.mysql.com/doc/sakila/en/). The processor CSV at /home/ga/Documents/imports/processor_transactions.csv is generated deterministically (random seed 42) from 6709 July 2005 payments in sakila.payment joined with sakila.customer emails. The CSV has 6719 data rows plus a header (6720 lines total, 723KB), with RFC 4180 format using \r\n line endings. CSV columns are: txn_id, merchant_ref, cust_email, amount, txn_date, status, notes. Data quality issues in the CSV: 327 rows have $ prefix in amounts (pid % 20 == 0), 190 rows have MM/DD/YYYY date format instead of YYYY-MM-DD (pid % 33 == 0), 141 rows have empty cust_email (pid % 50 == 0), and 3390 rows have commas inside the notes field (properly quoted per RFC 4180). The ground truth category breakdown is: MATCHED=6584, DATE_MISMATCH=50 (dates shifted +1 or +2 days), AMOUNT_MISMATCH=35 (amounts increased by $0.05-$0.49), INTERNAL_ONLY=40 (payments in Sakila but omitted from CSV), EXTERNAL_ONLY=30 (synthetic records with txn_ids TXN-EXT0000 through TXN-EXT0029, status='refund', 15 have fake emails refund_N@example.com and 15 have empty email), DUPLICATE_EXTERNAL=20 (same txn_id appearing twice in CSV with notes 'Duplicate, reprocessed'). Customer emails in Sakila are uppercase format FIRST.LAST@sakilacustomer.org, 599 distinct emails matching all 599 customers. July 2005 payment_ids range from 10 to 16042. Payment amounts are $0.99-$11.99 distributed across 12 tiers. MySQL secure_file_priv is set to /var/lib/mysql-files/ and local_infile is OFF, so LOAD DATA INFILE requires copying the CSV to /var/lib/mysql-files/ first. The agent must use LINES TERMINATED BY '\r\n' and OPTIONALLY ENCLOSED BY '"' for correct CSV import. The multi-match problem is real: some customers have up to 6 payments at the same amount in the same week (e.g., MAX.PITT@sakilacustomer.org has 6 payments of $4.99), requiring ROW_NUMBER() or similar deduplication in the matching logic. Expected deliverables: staging table processor_transactions with ~6719 rows and 7 columns, view v_payment_reconciliation with match_status/payment_id/txn_id/customer_id/internal_amount/external_amount/internal_date/external_date columns, stored procedure sp_apply_corrections, table audit_corrections with correction logs, table reconciliation_summary with 6 category rows, and two CSV exports in /home/ga/Documents/exports/. MySQL Workbench is running but has a known dbus-launch connection issue; the agent may need to use the mysql CLI client instead (user: ga, password: password123).
